name: Shenzhen
author: Kronifer
description:
深圳 (Shenzhen)

SZX · v1.0.0

Detailed map of the prefecture-level cities of 深圳 (Shenzhen), 东莞 (Dongguan), and 惠阳区 (Huiyang District, Huizhou) based off data from the 2023 5th National Economic Census.

Coverage

Playable Area: 6,500 km²

Population Summary

Total Population: 28,688,800

Modeled Demand: 20,442,339

Map Statistics

Buildings Indexed: 1,825,647

Demand Points: 9,626

Populations: 202,083

Mean Point Size: 2,123

Median Point Size: 931

Mean Pop Size: 101

Median Pop Size: 98

Special Demand

Airports

  • Shenzhen Bao'an International Airport: 101,136 daily arrivals/departures modeled

Universities

  • Shenzhen University: 35,455 students modeled

  • Chinese University of Hong Kong, Shenzhen Campus: 13,000 students modeled

  • MSU: 4,500 students modeled

  • PUSGS: 3,764 students modeled

Additional Features

  • Building Collision — A buildings index is included for all non-filtered buildings, assigning each a flat foundation depth of −10 m in-game. This provides underground collision geometry so that tunnels interact correctly with building footprints.

  • Neighborhood Labels — Three layers of place name labels are embedded in the map:

    • City labels — Prefecture-Level Cities, Districts

    • Municipality labels — Subdistricts, Counties, County-Level Cities

    • Neighborhood labels — Towns, Townships, Neighbourhoods, Villages

Methodology

This map was created using a multi-step methodology, sourcing data from the individual districts of Shenzhen, Huiyang District, and Dongguan (Dongguan has no county-level divisions).

Data Collection

5th Economic Census data was fetched from each individual District or City. Specifically, Communique/Bulletin no. 7, focusing on employees per subdistrict was obtained.

Spatial Assignment

Population and Employee data for each subdistrict based on the above bulletins were assigned to the geometry of each subdistrict using a Shapefile.

Geographical Processing - Stage 1

Data was disaggregated to the individual building level by assigning a building a usage metric based off of total commercial/residential volume within a Subdistrict.

Geographical Processing - Stage 2

Data was aggregated up to a hexagonal grid with side length n=400m for commute modelling.

Commute Modelling

O/D pairs were created using a Gravity Model, the source for which can be found in the sources linked below.

Map Data

Map Data was fetched from OpenStreetMap, Overture, and 3D-GloBFP and processed by RSlurry's depot package.

Sources

All data used in the creation of this map was obtained from freely available sources.

  • Depot: GitHub
  • 3D-GloBFP: Zenodo
  • Overture Maps
  • OpenStreetMap
